paper_title: Stable Isotope Analysis in Precipitation Samples from Varaždin, NW Croatia

methods.description: Process for collecting monthly composite precipitation samples using a specially designed rain gauge for stable isotope analysis.

steps.description: Monthly composite precipitation samples (N = 10) were collected using a specially designed rain gauge installed in the village Hrašćica.
steps.searchable_summary: collect monthly precipitation samples using a rain gauge

steps.description: The last day of the month, composite sample was poured into HDPE plastic bottles and preserved at 4 °C in a portable refrigerator until analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: store precipitation samples in HDPE bottles at 4°C

steps.description: To remove impurities, all samples were filtered through 0.45 μm sterile syringe filters before isotope analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: filter samples using 0.45 μm syringe filters

methods.description: Analysis of stable isotope ratios in precipitation using a Cavity Ring-Down Spectrometer for δ18O and δ2H.

steps.description: Stable isotope ratios in precipitation were analysed using Cavity Ring-Down Spectrometer Picarro L2130-i Isotope analyser.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze isotope ratios using Picarro L2130-i

steps.description: All measurements were checked with Picarro's standards and periodically against IAEA standards.
steps.searchable_summary: validate measurements with Picarro and IAEA standards

steps.description: Elimination of 'bad' samples or injections based on H2O concentrations, memory-effect correction, and normalization to VSMOW/SLAP scales.
steps.searchable_summary: process data for bad samples, memory-effect, and normalization

steps.description: The deuterium excess (d-excess) was calculated for each sample using the equation: d = δ2H - (8 × δ18O).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate deuterium excess using δ2H and δ18O
